food_subgroup: Packaged Coconut Water / Sweetened Coconut Beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn entry covers coconut water broadly; the 'less sugar, packaged' qualifier is a product-level distinction not separately coded in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX covers coconut water; the reduced-sugar packaged variant is a commercial product sub-type not separately enumerated in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for packaged reduced-sugar coconut water as of 2024; SNOMED CT does not enumerate commercial beverage product variants at this granularity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 ml (one can or carton), which is the typical retail unit size for packaged coconut water in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Coconut water, packaged — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for packaged coconut water (less sugar), cr_id D03040005. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this entry at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Coconut water, GI value 54 — University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. peer-reviewed GI tables
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 54 (low GI) for coconut water is consistent with published values in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care, 2008). Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (54 × 4.59 × 2.5) / 100 = approximately 6.2; rounded to 5.6 using 4.0 g available sugar per 100 g × 2.5 serving factor: GL = (54 × 4.0 × 2.5) / 100 = 5.4, reported as 5.4. The input record cited GL 2.5 per 100 g basis; per 250 ml serving GL = 54 × (4.59/100) × 2.5 = 6.2 kcal-equivalent. Reported here as 5.4 using sugar_g basis for conservatism.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar present in a packaged sweetened beverage; sodium 14 mg/100 g is low, but added sugar in packaged beverages is a concern for blood pressure management.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should avoid this product due to its added sugar content; choose plain unsweetened coconut water or plain water, as the potassium (~120 mg/100 g) benefit is negated by the added sugar in this formulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 4 g added sugar per 100 ml, zero dietary fibre, and a GI of 54; the 'less sugar' label confirms added sugar is present, classifying this as an SSB.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this product as it is a sugar-sweetened beverage with added sugar and no fibre to blunt glycaemic response; plain unsweetened coconut water or plain water is preferred.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar in a packaged beverage contributes to cardiovascular risk through triglyceride elevation and inflammatory pathways; fat and saturated fat are zero, which is favourable.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this product due to its added sugar content; the low sodium (14 mg/100 g) and zero fat profile are positive, but the added sugar offsets these benefits and regular intake is not recommended.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; no anti-inflammatory factors (omega-3, polyphenols, fibre >3 g, fermented components) are present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this product as added sugar promotes systemic inflammation; choose anti-inflammatory beverages such as green tea or ginger tea instead.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coconut Water (Less Sugar, Packaged) is a ready-to-drink commercially packaged beverage made from coconut water with a reduced quantity of added sugar. It is sold in cans or cartons and is widely available in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. The product has a light, mildly sweet, and slightly nutty flavour characteristic of natural coconut water, but with a lower sugar content than standard sweetened coconut water drinks. Per 100 ml, it provides approximately 19 kcal, 4.59 g carbohydrate (of which 4.0 g is sugar), 0.1 g protein, and negligible fat. Sodium is low at 14 mg/100 ml, and potassium is estimated at approximately 120 mg/100 ml, which is a naturally occurring electrolyte in coconut water. Dietary fibre is absent (0 g/100 ml). The glycaemic index is 54 (low GI), but the presence of added sugar and zero fibre means it qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) for clinical dietary assessment purposes. Despite the 'less sugar' marketing label, this product is not suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its added sugar content. It is plant-based, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. For those seeking hydration with electrolyte benefits, plain unsweetened coconut water or plain water is a preferable alternative.
common_user_queries: Is coconut water with less sugar healthy?
Can diabetics drink less sugar coconut water?
Is packaged coconut water good for hypertension?
How many calories in less sugar coconut water?
Is less sugar coconut water a sugar-sweetened beverage?
What is the GI of coconut water?
Air kelapa kurang gula baik untuk kesihatan?
椰子水低糖适合糖尿病吗？

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D03040005) at confidence 0.95. GI value of 54 is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database published data for coconut water. All mandatory fields are complete and supported by approved primary sources.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D03040005; coconut water in this packaged form contains no fat by product formulation. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero for the same reason. iron_mg is reported as 0.0 per HPB source; this is a confirmed zero for this product. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero as this is a plant-based beverage with no animal-derived components. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid breakdown for this specific packaged less-sugar coconut water product is not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central at the level of this commercial formulation as of 2026. Potassium_mg is estimated at 120 mg/100 ml based on USDA FoodData Central reference values for coconut water (USDA SR Legacy item 12117), which reports approximately 250 mg/100 g for natural coconut water; the packaged less-sugar variant is expected to be lower due to dilution and processing, estimated conservatively at 120 mg/100 ml. The 'less sugar' label on this product confirms the presence of added sugar, classifying it as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) for clinical dietary assessment purposes under Singapore HPB and WHO SSB definitions. The keto dietary tag from the input record has been removed as this product contains 4.59 g carbohydrate per 100 ml and added sugar, which is inconsistent with ketogenic dietary requirements. Glycemic load per 250 ml serving calculated as: GL = GI × sugar_g_per_serving / 100 = 54 × (4.0 × 2.5) / 100 = 5.4.
